title: ICFJ+ Fellowship 2026: Reporting on Literacy and Numeracy in Africa – Fully Funded Opportunity for Journalists in Kenya, Senegal, and South Africa
summary: The crisis in foundational learning across sub-Saharan Africa remains one of the most urgent yet underreported challenges facing the continent today. With an estimated 9 out of 10 children unable to read for meaning or perform basic mathematical operations by the age of 10, the implications for economic development, social mobility, and long-term national progress are profound. While evidence-based solutions to improve learning outcomes exist and political will is gradually strengthening, the pace of implementation across countries remains insufficient. Recognizing the powerful role of journalism in shaping public discourse and influencing policy, ICFJ+ has launched an impactful initiative aimed at strengthening education reporting across Africa. The Reporting on Literacy and Numeracy Fellowship 2026 is designed to equip journalists, content creators, and media professionals with the tools, knowledge, and support needed to produce compelling, data-driven, and solutions-oriented stories that can drive change. About the Fellowship Program This prestigious fellowship targets media professionals based in Kenya, Senegal, and South Africa, offering a unique opportunity to deepen their understanding of foundational learning challenges while enhancing their storytelling capacity. Over a six-month period, selected fellows will participate in a comprehensive program that combines training, mentorship, and hands-on reporting experience. The initiative focuses on improving coverage of literacy and numeracy issues, ensuring that reporting not only highlights problems but also explores practical solutions and amplifies voices from affected communities. What the Fellowship Offers Participants selected for the program will benefit from a range of valuable resources and opportunities, including: Reporting Grants: Financial support to produce in-depth, high-quality stories on literacy and numeracy. Individual Mentorship: Guidance from experienced journalists and subject-matter experts throughout the fellowship. Story Production Support: Editorial assistance to refine and publish impactful stories. Training Sessions: Capacity-building workshops focused on education data, pedagogy, and solutions journalism. Study Tours: Opportunities to gain exposure to successful education interventions and reporting practices. Professional Networking: Access to a network of like-minded journalists, educators, and policy influencers. The program is designed not just to enhance storytelling but to ensure that participants produce work that reaches key stakeholders, including policymakers, educators, and communities directly affected by learning gaps. Who Should Apply The fellowship is open to a diverse range of media professionals, including: Journalists working with established media organizations Freelance reporters Content creators and digital storytellers Social media influencers with a focus on public interest issues Applicants must be based in Kenya, Senegal, or South Africa and should demonstrate a strong interest in education, social policy, or development reporting. While prior experience in education reporting is an advantage, it is not a strict requirement. What Makes a Strong Application To stand out, applicants should clearly articulate: Their understanding of gaps in current coverage of foundational learning in their country A compelling story idea or reporting angle Their strategy for reaching and engaging policymakers, educators, and affected communities A commitment to producing impactful journalism beyond traditional publication formats The fellowship prioritizes individuals who are not only skilled storytellers but also passionate about driving meaningful change through their work. Application Timeline Applications Open: April 10, 2026 Application Deadline: May 10, 2026 (11:59 PM EST) Interested applicants are encouraged to apply early and ensure that all required materials are submitted before the deadline. Why This Fellowship Matters At its core, this initiative seeks to transform how foundational learning issues are reported in Africa. By empowering journalists with data literacy, storytelling tools, and access to expert insights, the program aims to elevate education reporting from surface-level narratives to impactful journalism that informs, influences, and inspires action. In a region where millions of children are at risk of being left behind, the role of the media is critical. This fellowship represents an opportunity for journalists to become catalysts for change, using their platforms to spotlight solutions, hold systems accountable, and ultimately contribute to improving learning outcomes across the continent. Application Deadline: May 10, 2026 (11:59 PM EST) APPLY HERE For more information about this opportunity, click here For more global opportunities, click here The post ICFJ+ Fellowship 2026: Reporting on Literacy and Numeracy in Africa – Fully Funded Opportunity for Journalists in Kenya, Senegal, and South Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Research Experience Placements (REPs) Summer 2026 at the University of Edinburgh: Paid Research Opportunity for Students
summary: If you re an undergraduate or integrated Master s student looking to gain hands-on research experience this summer, the Research Experience Placements (REPs) for Summer 2026 at the University of Edinburgh present an outstanding opportunity. Funded by the Natural Environment Research Council (NERC) under the E5 Doctoral Training Partnership, these placements are designed to help students explore careers in environmental sciences through real-world research projects. Why You Should Apply The REPs program offers more than just a summer activity—it provides a meaningful step toward a future in research or postgraduate study. Participants will: Work on real research projects supervised by leading academics Receive mentorship from current PhD students Gain practical skills in environmental science research Build confidence and clarify career pathways Earn a salary above the National Living Wage This is a highly valuable experience, particularly for students considering postgraduate study or research-focused careers. Placement Overview Duration: 7–8 weeks (June–August 2026) Working Options: Full-time or part-time Salary: Paid at University of Edinburgh Grade 3.1 (pro-rata from £24,729/year) Location: Edinburgh and partner institutions including the UK Centre for Ecology Hydrology (UKCEH) and the Royal Botanic Garden Edinburgh Start Date: Earliest start from 15 June 2026 Please note that participants must be physically based in the UK for the duration of the placement. Research Projects Available Students can apply to one project only. Topics include: Wildlife ecology and animal behavior Soil chemistry and hydrogen measurement Climate and glacier research Plant taxonomy and biodiversity Agricultural sustainability Marine ecology and oxygen dynamics Conservation strategies for tropical forests Each project is supervised by an expert in the field, offering a unique opportunity to contribute to impactful research. Eligibility Criteria To be eligible, applicants must: Be enrolled in their first undergraduate degree (including integrated Master s) at a UK Higher Education Institution Not have previously completed a NERC REP Meet NERC PhD funding eligibility requirements Have the right to work or study in the UK for the full placement duration Students in their final year are eligible if they have not graduated before the placement begins. Commitment to Inclusion The program strongly encourages applications from students from underrepresented backgrounds, including: Ethnic minority groups Students with disabilities Individuals from low-income backgrounds Applicants may submit a Widening Participation statement to provide context about their experiences. Important Considerations No funding is provided for relocation or accommodation Applicants must ensure they can commute to the placement location A final report must be submitted at the end of the program Application Process To apply, submit the following documents via email: Completed application form Academic reference Interim academic transcript Equality and Diversity Monitoring form Optional: Widening Participation statement Deadline: 12:00 Noon (UK time), Friday 1 May 2026 Applications must be complete at the time of submission. How to Apply Find full details and application materials here: APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more internship opportunities, click here . Final Thoughts The Research Experience Placements at the University of Edinburgh offer a rare chance to immerse yourself in meaningful research while earning a salary. With limited placements available, early preparation is essential. If you re serious about a future in environmental science or research, this opportunity could be a turning point. The post Research Experience Placements (REPs) Summer 2026 at the University of Edinburgh: Paid Research Opportunity for Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Master of Public Administration (MPA) at the African School of Governance (ASG): A Transformative Leadership Programme for Africa’s Future
summary: Shape Governance. Drive Impact. Lead Africa Forward. If you are passionate about transforming public systems, influencing policy, and leading with purpose, the Master of Public Administration (MPA) at the African School of Governance (ASG) offers a powerful pathway to impact. Designed specifically for Africa’s realities, this programme prepares a new generation of leaders equipped to tackle the continent’s most pressing governance and development challenges. With a strong emphasis on leadership, innovation, and practical application, this two-year graduate programme goes beyond traditional academic learning to cultivate ethical, strategic, and action-oriented public leaders. 🎓 Programme Overview The ASG MPA is a two-year, 120-credit programme structured to build both foundational knowledge and advanced leadership capacity. Year 1: Foundations of Leadership, Policy, and Institutions Students develop a solid grounding in: Public policy analysis Institutional economics Political economy Research methods Leadership for public service Outcome: A deep understanding of how governance systems, leadership, and economic structures influence development outcomes across Africa. Year 2: Specialization, Strategy, and Impact Students move into advanced learning and application through: Specialized tracks Policy-focused coursework Real-world engagement 🌱 Experiential Learning Opportunities Work Integrated Learning (WIL) Students gain hands-on experience through placements in: Government ministries NGOs Regional organizations Private-sector institutions Capstone Project A supervised project where students: Develop policy solutions Propose institutional reforms Conduct applied research 💡 Key Skills and Competencies Developed Graduates of the ASG MPA programme will: Lead with integrity and a pan-African mindset Design and implement evidence-based policies Navigate complex governance and political systems Manage public institutions effectively Drive inclusive and accountable governance 🌟 Why Choose the ASG MPA? Africa-Centered Curriculum Rooted in African contexts, histories, and development priorities. Leadership Development Focus Emphasizes ethical leadership, purpose-driven action, and public service values. Hands-On Learning Approach Combines case studies, simulations, and fieldwork for real-world readiness. Global Faculty Partnerships Learn from leading African and international experts. Pan-African Network Join a vibrant community of emerging leaders across the continent. 💰 Scholarships Available Both merit-based and need-based scholarships are offered to support qualified candidates. 📝 Application Process Steps to Apply Select your programme from the official portal Start your application Complete all required information Submit your application Receive your Application ID via email Proceed with payment Track your application status 📄 Required Documents National ID (for Rwandans aged 18+) Valid passport (for non-Rwandans) Bachelor’s degree certificate Academic transcripts Updated CV/Resume Proof of English proficiency (if applicable) 💳 Application Fee $50 USD (non-refundable) 📅 Application Deadline 15 May 2026 APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more scholarship opportunities, click here . 📧 Support For assistance, contact: support@asg.ac The post Master of Public Administration (MPA) at the African School of Governance (ASG): A Transformative Leadership Programme for Africa’s Future appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Remote Research Officer Job at Southern Voice (2026) – Earn Up to $2,200/Month
summary: If you’re passionate about global development, policy research, and amplifying voices from the Global South, this opportunity could be exactly what you’ve been waiting for. Southern Voice is currently hiring a Research Officer to join its dynamic international team in a fully remote capacity. This role offers a unique chance to work at the intersection of research, policy, and global collaboration—supporting impactful, multi-country initiatives that influence global development conversations. About Southern Voice Southern Voice is a network of 71 think tanks across the Global South, committed to generating high-quality, evidence-based research to inform global policy dialogues, especially around the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). The organization plays a critical role in ensuring that perspectives from the Global South shape international decision-making processes. Position Overview The Research Officer will work closely with the Head of Research Innovation to coordinate and implement research initiatives, manage publications, and contribute to knowledge production. This is a remote, full-time consultancy role with a strong focus on research coordination, stakeholder engagement, and policy-relevant outputs. Location: Remote (Home-based) Start Date: June 8, 2026 (earliest) Contract Duration: Initial 3-month probation, followed by a 1-year consultancy (renewable) Salary: USD 2,000 – 2,200 per month Key Responsibilities The selected candidate will: Coordinate multi-country research projects and initiatives Produce high-quality research outputs (reports, briefs, concept notes) Manage publication processes including peer review and dissemination Organize meetings, workshops, and collaborative activities Engage with global stakeholders and research partners Support institutional planning and strategic research development Contribute to partnerships and global policy dialogues Why You Should Apply Working with Southern Voice means being part of a globally recognized network influencing development policy. This role provides: Exposure to international policy and research ecosystems Opportunities to collaborate with global experts and institutions A platform to amplify Global South perspectives Hands-on experience in impactful, policy-driven research Requirements To be considered, applicants should have: A Bachelor’s degree in International Development, Political Science, Economics, or related fields At least 3 years of experience in research or policy-related roles Strong analytical, writing, and coordination skills Experience managing research projects or initiatives Excellent English communication skills Ability to work across time zones (minimum 5-hour overlap with UTC+5:30) Application Process Applicants must submit: A 2-page CV A 1-page cover letter (in English) addressing: Why you are a strong fit Your understanding of Southern Voice’s work How your experience aligns with the role Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, so early submission is highly encouraged. Deadline May 8, 2026 (12:00 PM CET) Note: The position may be filled before the deadline due to rolling review. How to Apply APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more job opportunities, click here . The post Remote Research Officer Job at Southern Voice (2026) – Earn Up to $2,200/Month appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Peering Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Global Opportunity for Internet Infrastructure Professionals
summary: The 2026 Peering Fellowship is now open for applications, offering a unique opportunity for Internet infrastructure professionals to strengthen their expertise, expand global networks, and contribute to building a more resilient and accessible Internet ecosystem. Launched by the Internet Society, this six-month fellowship program is designed to empower professionals working in peering and interconnection with the tools, knowledge, and global exposure needed to tackle real-world Internet challenges. Application Deadline Applications close on 17 May 2026 at 23:59 UTC . Early application is strongly encouraged. About the Peering Fellowship In an increasingly interconnected world, the efficiency and resilience of the Internet depend heavily on strong peering and interconnection systems. The Peering Fellowship supports professionals working to improve Internet access by enhancing local interconnection infrastructure and advocating for supportive policy environments. This program selects 20 fellows annually and provides a structured blend of virtual learning and in-person engagement at major regional peering events. Program Structure and What to Expect Participants in the Peering Fellowship will engage in a comprehensive learning experience that includes: Virtual Learning Sessions: Interactive online sessions, workshops, and collaborative discussions In-Person Peering Event Attendance: Fellows will attend a regional peering event aligned with their location Technical Training: Deep dives into Internet routing, Internet Exchange Points (IXPs), and interconnection strategies Policy and Advocacy Training: Understanding the regulatory landscape shaping Internet access Networking Opportunities: Direct engagement with global industry leaders and peers Local Impact Projects: Fellows will apply their learning to improve Internet infrastructure in their communities Fellowship Benefits The Peering Fellowship offers both professional and financial support, including: Round-trip airfare to a regional peering event Hotel accommodation during the event Stipend for additional expenses Access to a global network of Internet professionals Advanced training from experienced industry experts Opportunity to contribute to improving Internet access and resilience Key Program Timeline (2026) 27 April: Applications open 17 May: Applications close 18 May – 19 June: Application review period Late June: Selection notifications July – December: Fellowship program duration August – November: Regional peering events December: Program concludes Regional Peering Events Fellows will attend one of the following events based on their region: Africa: AfPIF (18–21 August, Kigali, Rwanda) Latin America Caribbean: LACNIC/LACNOG (19–22 October, Mendoza, Argentina) Asia-Pacific: Peering Asia (2–6 November, Osaka, Japan) Middle East: Event to be announced Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Be based in Africa, Asia-Pacific, Latin America and the Caribbean, or the Middle East Have at least 3 years of experience in Internet infrastructure or related fields Possess knowledge or involvement in routing, IXPs, peering, or Internet policy Be able to commit 4–6 hours weekly throughout the program Have reliable Internet access and necessary devices Be available for sessions between 12:00 and 16:00 UTC Demonstrate basic English communication skills Not have participated in recent Internet Society fellowships (2024–present) Selection Process Applications will undergo a two-stage review process conducted by independent committees. The process is anonymized, ensuring fairness and impartiality. All applicants will receive a notification regarding their selection status after the review period. Why You Should Apply The Peering Fellowship is more than just a training program—it’s a gateway to becoming part of a global movement dedicated to improving Internet accessibility, affordability, and resilience. Fellows gain practical experience, expand their professional networks, and contribute to meaningful change in their regions. How to Apply Interested applicants should prepare their submissions before the deadline and ensure they meet all eligibility requirements. For inquiries, applicants can reach out via email: fellowships@isoc.org . APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here . The post Peering Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Global Opportunity for Internet Infrastructure Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: FutureSwell Creator Lab 2026: A Global Accelerator Empowering Ocean Storytellers to Drive Climate Action
summary: The urgency of ocean conservation has never been greater—and neither has the power of storytelling. Recognizing the influence of digital creators in shaping public opinion and mobilizing communities, FutureSwell has launched its inaugural Creator Lab 2026 , an 8-week virtual accelerator designed to equip ocean-focused creators with the tools, mentorship, and strategic clarity needed to drive real-world impact. This program is more than just a training experience—it is a movement to amplify voices, align narratives, and catalyze collective action for the future of our oceans. About FutureSwell FutureSwell is a global storytelling platform and consultancy founded in 2020 with a mission to increase public engagement in ocean climate issues. Through modern media, partnerships, and community-building initiatives, FutureSwell works to: Amplify ocean-based solutions Elevate diverse community voices Advocate for systemic environmental change By combining storytelling with strategic advocacy, FutureSwell creates accessible pathways for individuals from all backgrounds to participate in ocean conservation efforts. What is the Creator Lab? The FutureSwell Creator Lab is an intensive, fully virtual, and free 8-week accelerator program aimed at empowering ocean conservation content creators. It focuses on transforming passionate storytellers into impactful campaigners who can shape narratives and influence global conversations around ocean climate issues. Participants will engage in a structured learning journey that blends education, mentorship, and hands-on project development. Program Highlights Throughout the 2-month incubator, selected creators will: 1. Develop a Narrative Project Create and publish a storytelling project aligned with a specific ocean climate campaign focus Collaborate with FutureSwell to ensure strategic impact and visibility 2. Receive Expert Mentorship Learn directly from leaders in ocean conservation, media, and campaigning Gain insights into effective communication strategies and audience engagement 3. Build Campaign Strategy Skills Understand how to align personal storytelling with broader environmental goals Learn how to mobilize audiences and influence public perception 4. Join a Global Community Connect with like-minded ocean storytellers from around the world Become part of a collaborative network committed to long-term impact 5. Gain Long-Term Clarity Define your role within the global ocean conservation movement Leave the program with a clear direction for future content and campaigns Learning Experience The Creator Lab combines multiple learning formats to ensure a comprehensive experience: Live training seminars Interactive workshops One-on-one and group mentorship Peer collaboration and feedback Real-world project execution This hybrid approach ensures that participants not only learn but also apply their knowledge in meaningful ways. Who Should Apply? FutureSwell is looking for creators who are: Passionate about ocean conservation and climate issues Active on social media or digital platforms Interested in using storytelling as a tool for change Eager to grow their skills in campaigning and communication Committed to contributing to a collective mission No specific professional background is required—FutureSwell believes conservation is for everyone. What You Will Gain By the end of the program, participants will: Have a published narrative project with real-world impact Gain advanced storytelling and campaign strategy skills Build connections with global experts and peers Strengthen their personal brand as an ocean advocate Be equipped to drive systemic change through content Application Details Program Duration: 8 weeks (virtual) Cost: Free Application Deadline: May 15, 2026 Review Process: Rolling basis (applications may close early once spots are filled) Why This Matters In today’s digital age, creators are not just influencers—they are changemakers. They shape conversations, influence culture, and mobilize communities. The FutureSwell Creator Lab recognizes this power and seeks to harness it for one of the most critical causes of our time: protecting our oceans. By training and supporting creators, FutureSwell is building a global network of storytellers who can drive awareness, inspire action, and contribute to a sustainable future. How to Apply Interested candidates can apply through the official application portal HERE . Click HERE for more information about the FutureSwell Creator Lab 2026. Explore more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here . The post FutureSwell Creator Lab 2026: A Global Accelerator Empowering Ocean Storytellers to Drive Climate Action appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Caribbean Development Bank Young Professionals Programme 2026–2028 (Fully Paid)
summary: The Caribbean Development Bank (CDB) has officially opened applications for its Young Professionals Programme (YPP) 2026–2028 —a highly competitive, fully paid opportunity for early-career professionals looking to build a global career in international development. This prestigious programme offers a two-year contract in Barbados , hands-on experience across key development sectors, and the chance to work with one of the Caribbean’s leading multilateral institutions focused on sustainable growth and resilience. About the CDB Young Professionals Programme The CDB Young Professionals Programme is designed to recruit and develop high-potential young professionals (under 32 years old) with strong academic backgrounds and relevant work experience. Participants gain exposure to the Bank’s operations, policies, and development initiatives while contributing to impactful regional projects. The programme typically runs for two years , offering structured learning, real-world assignments, and career progression opportunities . Available Roles for 2026–2028 Cohort For the 2026 intake, CDB is recruiting four young professionals across the following areas: Human Resources Data Analyst (Human Resource Management, Data Analytics, Statistics, Business Administration, Information Systems, Economics, or related fields) Digital Communications (Communications, Media Production, Digital Media, Journalism, Graphic Design, or related fields) Data Analytics and Information Management (Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, Data Science, or related disciplines) Private Sector Development (Economics, Finance, Business Administration, International Development, Public Policy, or related fields) Salary and Benefits Successful candidates will receive a competitive international package , including: Starting salary: USD $60,600 per year 6 weeks paid annual leave Comprehensive medical insurance Full relocation support (including family relocation) Placement at CDB Headquarters in Barbados This aligns with global standards for multilateral development banks, where young professionals programmes often combine strong compensation with structured career development pathways. Eligibility Criteria To apply, candidates must meet the following requirements: Be under 32 years old by December 31, 2026 Be a national of a CDB member country Hold a relevant degree (typically at least a master’s level) Have a minimum of 2 years of relevant professional experience Demonstrate strong interest in international development and regional impact The programme targets candidates ready to transition into impactful careers within development institutions. Eligible Countries The Caribbean Development Bank (CDB) has 28 member countries , and you must be a national of one of these to apply for programmes like the Young Professionals Programme. Here’s the full list: CDB Member Countries 🇦🇮 Anguilla 🇦🇬 Antigua and Barbuda 🇧🇸 Bahamas 🇧🇧 Barbados 🇧🇿 Belize 🇧🇷 Brazil 🇻🇬 British Virgin Islands 🇨🇦 Canada 🇰🇾 Cayman Islands 🇨🇳 China 🇨🇴 Colombia 🇩🇲 Dominica 🇩🇪 Germany 🇬🇩 Grenada 🇬🇾 Guyana 🇭🇹 Haiti 🇮🇹 Italy 🇯🇲 Jamaica 🇲🇽 Mexico 🇲🇸 Montserrat 🇱🇨 Saint Lucia 🇰🇳 Saint Kitts and Nevis 🇻🇨 Saint Vincent and the Grenadines 🇸🇷 Suriname 🇹🇹 Trinidad and Tobago 🇹🇨 Turks and Caicos Islands 🇬🇧 United Kingdom 🇻🇪 Venezuela Why You Should Apply The CDB Young Professionals Programme is one of the top entry routes into international development careers , offering: Direct experience in a multilateral development bank Exposure to regional policy, finance, and project implementation Opportunity to work on youth development, climate resilience, and economic growth A strong pathway into long-term international careers CDB places a strong emphasis on youth empowerment and capacity building, recognizing young professionals as key drivers of sustainable development across the Caribbean . Application Deadline Deadline: May 15, 2026 👉 Apply here: https://career5.successfactors.eu/career?company=caribbeandP2 career_ns=job_listing_summary 👉 Find out more here https://www.caribank.org/work-with-us/careers Young Professional, Digital Communications Application Deadline Friday, May 15, 2026 23:59 The role spans digital content development, video and audio production, graphic design, and cross‑platform storytelling and event production support. It ensures that high‑quality, brand‑aligned content is created for internal and external audiences across websites, social media, digital campaigns, and multimedia channels. Young Professional, Human Resources Data Analyst Application Deadline Friday, May 15, 2026 23:59 The role assists in collecting, analysing, and interpreting HR data from multiple sources to support workforce planning, talent management, and organisational performance monitoring. The incumbent will also contribute to the development of HR dashboards, reports, and data visualisations that provide timely insights into workforce metrics and trends, spanning the breadth of the HRD. Young Professional, Private Sector Application Deadline Friday, May 15, 2026 23:59 The role is designed to provide broad exposure to private sector development operations and policy work, enabling the YP to contribute meaningfully to CDB’s strategic objectives while developing professional expertise in investment finance, project management, and development banking. Young Professional, Data Analytics and Information Management Application Deadline Friday, May 15, 2026 23:59 The Young Professional (YP), Data Analytics and Information Management role supports the Bank’s data analytics and information governance work by helping develop dashboards and reports, collecting/validating data, maintaining KPI and metadata documentation, and contributing to data governance artifacts (e.g., data dictionaries and glossaries). The YP also coordinates meetings and training, completes progress updates and research on improved tools and practices, and provides first-level support to users while building professional capability through on-the-job learning and mentoring. Pro Tip Tailor your application to demonstrate: Strong analytical or technical skills relevant to the role Clear interest in Caribbean or global development issues Evidence of impact, leadership, or problem-solving in your past work Multilateral institutions look for candidates who combine technical expertise with a clear development mindset —so make that explicit in your CV and cover letter. Final Thoughts If you’re looking to break into global development, policy, or international finance, the Caribbean Development Bank Young Professionals Programme 2026–2028 is a high-impact, career-defining opportunity . With competitive benefits, global exposure, and meaningful work, this programme offers a powerful launchpad into the world of international development. title: New Development Bank Internship Programme 2026 (Shanghai, China) – Apply by June 30
summary: Are you looking to build a career in international development, finance, or sustainable infrastructure ? The New Development Bank Internship Programme 2026 is now open for applications, offering a unique opportunity to gain hands-on experience in a global, multicultural environment. This prestigious internship is based in Shanghai, China , and is open to young professionals from across NDB member countries. Key Summary Host Organization: New Development Bank Programme: Internship Programme 2026 Location: Shanghai, China Deadline: June 30, 2026 (11:59 PM CST) Eligibility: Nationals of NDB member countries, aged 30 or below Experience Level: Maximum 1 year of full-time work experience Education: Master’s completed within last 12 months OR currently enrolled Language: English (required) About the New Development Bank (NDB) The New Development Bank (NDB) is a multilateral development bank established by the BRICS countries—Brazil, Russia, India, China, and South Africa. Its mission is to mobilize resources for infrastructure and sustainable development projects across emerging markets and developing countries (EMDCs). Through its internship programme, NDB aims to nurture the next generation of global development professionals . NDB Member Countries To be eligible, applicants must be nationals of one of the NDB member countries: Brazil Russia India China South Africa Bangladesh United Arab Emirates Egypt Note: Membership may expand—always confirm on the official portal. Internship Overview Location: Shanghai, China Programme Type: Full-time Internship Target Applicants: Nationals of NDB member countries Application Deadline: June 30, 2026 (11:59 PM China Standard Time) What You’ll Do (Key Responsibilities) As an NDB intern, you will gain real-world experience by supporting teams across different departments. Your responsibilities may include: Providing administrative support (data entry, scheduling, documentation) Conducting research and data analysis Assisting with projects and departmental initiatives Supporting report writing and documentation Participating in team meetings and presentations Engaging in training and professional development Collaborating with cross-functional teams Supporting presentations and stakeholder materials Handling ad hoc assignments and special tasks Responsibilities may vary depending on the department. Eligibility Criteria To apply for the NDB Internship Programme 2026, you must: Be a national of an NDB member country Be 30 years old or younger Have fluency in English Have no more than 1 year of full-time work experience Have: Completed a postgraduate (Master’s) degree within the last 12 months , OR Be currently enrolled in a full-time postgraduate programme Be available to work full-time during the internship Why Apply? (Benefits) Work at a leading multilateral development bank Collaborate with international experts Gain experience in development finance and infrastructure Build strong research, analytical, and professional skills Strengthen your pathway into UN, World Bank, and global careers Life at the New Development Bank NDB offers a dynamic, diverse, and forward-looking work environment : Innovative: Encourages new ideas and fresh thinking Diverse: Inclusive culture with global talent Forward-Looking: Focused on sustainable development impact How to Apply Ready to apply? Use the official link below: 👉 Apply Here: https://career15.sapsf.cn/career?career%5fns=job%5flisting company=newdevelop navBarLevel=JOB%5fSEARCH rcm%5fsite%5flocale=en%5fUS career_job_req_id=1780 selected_lang=en_US 🔗 Explore our careers page for more details: www.ndb.int/careers 📌 Note: Applicants based in Russia experiencing technical issues can send their CV and cover letter to career@ndb.int Pro Tip for Applicants To stand out: Highlight coursework or research in development, finance, or economics Showcase analytical, research, and communication skills Include relevant academic projects, internships, or volunteer work Final Thoughts The New Development Bank Internship Programme 2026 is a high-impact global opportunity for young graduates ready to step into international development and finance. If you meet the criteria, this is one opportunity you don’t want to miss. title: LSE-Miguel Dols Fellowship 2026–2027: Complete Guide to Eligibility, Application Process, Research Focus & How to Apply
summary: The London School of Economics and Political Science (LSE), through its Cañada Blanch Centre , is inviting applications for the highly regarded LSE-Miguel Dols Fellowship for the Autumn Term 2026–2027 . This prestigious academic opportunity is designed for scholars seeking to deepen their research, collaborate internationally, and engage with one of the world’s leading social science institutions. If you are an academic, PhD researcher, or established scholar working on globally relevant topics, this fellowship provides a valuable platform to advance your work within a dynamic intellectual environment. What is the LSE-Miguel Dols Fellowship? The LSE-Miguel Dols Fellowship is a short-term academic residency program hosted at the Cañada Blanch Centre at LSE in London. It offers selected fellows the opportunity to: Conduct independent research Access LSE’s world-class academic resources Engage with scholars and researchers across disciplines Contribute to ongoing academic discussions and initiatives The fellowship is highly selective, with only a small cohort of 3 to 4 fellows admitted each year, ensuring a focused and high-impact academic experience. Fellowship Duration and Location Program Period: Monday, 28 September – Friday, 11 December 2026 Location: London, United Kingdom Host Institution: London School of Economics and Political Science During this period, fellows will be embedded within the academic ecosystem of LSE, benefiting from its global reputation in research and policy influence. Important Deadline Application Deadline: 1 June 2026 Applicants are strongly encouraged to submit their materials well in advance to ensure full consideration. What Does the Fellowship Offer? While the LSE-Miguel Dols Fellowship is not a funded program, it provides substantial academic and professional benefits: 1. Access to World-Class Research Facilities Full access to LSE libraries and academic resources Exposure to cutting-edge research tools and data 2. Academic Collaboration Opportunities Interaction with leading scholars and researchers Participation in seminars, lectures, and academic events 3. International Research Exposure Opportunity to conduct research in a globally recognized institution Build cross-border academic networks 4. Prestige and Academic Recognition Enhance your academic profile by affiliating with LSE Strengthen your research credibility and visibility Funding Information It is important to note that: The fellowship does not provide financial support Selected fellows must secure funding independently through: Home institutions External grants Personal resources This makes it essential for applicants to plan their finances before applying. Research Areas and Priority Topics The fellowship welcomes a broad range of research topics, particularly those aligned with global challenges and policy-relevant issues. Preference is given to proposals addressing: Sustainable Development Climate Change and Environmental Policy Inequality and Social Justice Demographic Change and Migration Innovation, Technology, and Productivity Governance, Democracy, and Public Policy European Integration and Regional Cooperation Education and Employment Trends Public Health Challenges Gender and Ethnic Inequality Additionally, projects that bridge research between Spain and the United Kingdom are particularly encouraged. Who Can Apply? The fellowship is open to a wide range of academic professionals, including: Early-career researchers PhD candidates Established academics and professors Applicants should be actively engaged in research relevant to the Centre’s thematic focus areas. Application Requirements To apply, candidates must submit the following: Required Documents Curriculum Vitae (CV) Research Proposal (Maximum 600 Words) Your research proposal should clearly outline: Your research topic and objectives Methodology Expected outcomes Relevance to the Centre’s focus areas How to Apply Follow these steps to complete your application: Step 1: Prepare Your Documents Ensure your CV and research proposal are clear, concise, and aligned with the fellowship priorities. Step 2: Submit via Email Send your application to: m.osuna-vergara@lse.ac.uk Step 3: Use the Correct Subject Line Subject: LSE-Miguel Dols Fellowship Application Step 4: Review Guidelines Applicants are encouraged to review detailed guidelines available on the official LSE webpage before submission. Selection Process The selection committee evaluates applications based on: Academic excellence Relevance of research topic Potential impact of the research Alignment with the Centre’s mission Contribution to academic discourse Due to the limited number of fellowships available, competition is expected to be high. Why You Should Apply The LSE-Miguel Dols Fellowship offers a unique opportunity to: Advance your research in a globally respected institution Build international academic collaborations Gain access to top-tier research infrastructure Enhance your academic and professional trajectory For scholars looking to position themselves at the forefront of global policy and research discussions, this fellowship is a strategic opportunity. Final Thoughts The LSE-Miguel Dols Fellowship 2026–2027 stands out as a prestigious academic residency designed for researchers committed to addressing some of the world’s most pressing challenges. Although it does not provide direct funding, the intellectual, institutional, and networking benefits make it an invaluable opportunity for serious academics aiming to elevate their research and global impact. If your research aligns with global policy issues and you are ready to engage with a world-class academic community, this fellowship is well worth pursuing. For more information about this fellowship visit HERE . title: UNICEF Philippines Social and Behavior Change (SBC) Officer Opportunity 2026 – Full-Time National Volunteer Role in Manila
summary: If you are passionate about creating meaningful social impact, influencing positive behavioral change, and contributing to children’s welfare, this opportunity with UNICEF Philippines could be your next career breakthrough. The Social and Behavior Change (SBC) Officer role offers a unique chance to work at the intersection of communication, behavioral science, and development programming while supporting national priorities and global Sustainable Development Goals. Overview of the Opportunity The Social and Behavior Change (SBC) Officer position is a full-time, onsite national UN Volunteer assignment based in Manila, Philippines. This 12-month role (with the possibility of extension) is designed for professionals eager to support UNICEF’s mission of promoting and protecting children’s rights, especially for the most vulnerable populations. Location: Manila, Philippines Start Date: 25 May 2026 Duration: 12 months (extendable) Work Schedule: Full-time Volunteer Type: National Specialist (UN Volunteer) Work Mode: Onsite (UN premises) Application Deadline: 12 May 2026 About UNICEF’s Mission and SBC Approach UNICEF works globally to ensure every child thrives, focusing on health, education, protection, and equality. A key strategy in achieving these outcomes is Social and Behavior Change (SBC), which aims to: Strengthen community engagement Improve service uptake Influence positive behaviors and social norms Address barriers related to knowledge, attitudes, and practices The SBC Officer will play a central role in advancing these objectives by supporting evidence-based strategies and fostering collaboration across sectors. Key Responsibilities and Duties The role is structured across five major functional areas: 1. Capacity Development for SBC Facilitate learning programs and training sessions for UNICEF staff and partners Conduct training needs assessments and evaluate learning outcomes Maintain detailed documentation and reporting of capacity-building activities 2. Technical Support to Programme Sectors Provide strategic and operational SBC support across sectors such as health, education, and child protection Assist in designing, implementing, and monitoring SBC interventions Conduct quality assurance reviews of program materials and plans 3. SBC Systems Strengthening and Integration Support policy dialogues and multi-stakeholder engagement Contribute to integrating SBC into academic and professional learning systems Prepare concept notes, reports, and meeting summaries 4. Knowledge Management and Network Engagement Develop knowledge products such as case studies, reports, and presentations Support participation in conferences and workshops Manage and organize SBC knowledge repositories 5. Monitoring, Reporting, and Programme Learning Track program results and compile reports Analyze data to improve program effectiveness Document lessons learned for continuous improvement Volunteerism and Additional Contributions As a UN Volunteer, you are also expected to: Promote volunteerism and community engagement Participate in UNV events such as International Volunteer Day Contribute articles and reports on field experiences Support onboarding of new UN Volunteers Encourage the use of online volunteering platforms Required Qualifications and Experience Education Bachelor’s degree in fields such as: Development Communication Behavioral Science Sociology, Psychology, Anthropology Communication for Development or related disciplines Advanced degrees are an added advantage Experience Minimum of 3 years in: Social and Behavior Change Communication for Development Program management or related fields Experience in emergency or humanitarian settings is preferred Languages Fluent in English and Filipino Core Competencies Successful candidates will demonstrate: Strong technical knowledge of SBC strategies High ethical standards and integrity Excellent teamwork and respect for diversity Commitment to continuous learning Strong planning, communication, and analytical skills Flexibility and adaptability in dynamic environments Benefits and Entitlements As a National UN Volunteer, you will receive: Monthly Volunteer Living Allowance (VLA) Entry and Exit Lump Sum Allowances Comprehensive insurance coverage Annual leave (2.5 days per month) Access to UN learning and career development resources Living Conditions Volunteers are responsible for arranging their own accommodation and daily living needs. Manila offers access to essential services including healthcare, markets, and communication infrastructure. Important Notes UNICEF is an equal opportunity organization committed to diversity and inclusion Reasonable accommodations are available for persons with disabilities COVID-19 vaccination is required for onsite roles No fees are charged at any stage of the recruitment process How to Apply Interested candidates should apply through the official UN Volunteer platform before the deadline. Ensure your application highlights relevant experience, technical skills, and alignment with UNICEF’s mission. For more information about this opportunity, visit here .
